title: cherleria capillacea
intro: Cherleria capillacea, also known as Bistort or Alpine Bistort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Polygonaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, including diarrhea and ulcers, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic. Cherleria capillacea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried powder for medicinal use, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
